title: Lemon Bars
tagline: Buttery shortbread crust, tangy lemon filling, powdered sugar snow. Perfect.
description: Classic lemon bars with a crisp shortbread base and silky, tart lemon curd topping. The ideal balance of sweet and sour.
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a 9x13 inch pan with parchment, leaving overhang for easy removal.
Make the crust: Beat butter and powdered sugar until fluffy. Add flour and salt, mix until crumbly. Press evenly into pan.
Bake crust 18-20 minutes until edges are light golden. Don't overbake—it continues cooking with the filling.
Make the filling: Whisk eggs and sugar until smooth. Add flour, lemon juice, and zest. Whisk until combined.
Pour filling over hot crust. Return to oven and bake 22-25 minutes until filling is set and no longer jiggles.
Cool completely in pan. Refrigerate at least 2 hours.
Lift out using parchment. Dust generously with powdered sugar. Cut into bars.
notes: Fresh lemons only: Bottled juice doesn't compare. You need that bright, fresh flavor.
Hot crust: Pour filling onto hot crust to help it set properly.
Clean cuts: Wipe knife between cuts. Use a thin, sharp blade.
Storage: Refrigerate up to 5 days. Dust with powdered sugar just before serving.
Room temp: Let sit 10 minutes before serving for best texture.
